authoritative_sources.title: AAF Technical Information and Specification Library
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AAF Association is the originating standards body for AAF and its specification library is authoritative for editorial interchange artifacts used in post turnovers.
used_for: interchange boundary between editorial and downstream finishing teams

authoritative_sources.title: AMWA Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AMWA is a recognized media-workflow standards body and explicitly positions AS-11 as the delivery specification family for finished media files.

authoritative_sources.title: Specifications & Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DPP is a recognized content-supply-chain operating body whose public guidance names air-ready masters, post-production best practices, metadata exchange, delivery requirements, and the need to defer to the commissioning broadcaster or platform.
used_for: air-ready master and delivery requirement terminology

authoritative_sources.title: SMPTE ST 2067 — Interoperable Master Format (IMF)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SMPTE is the primary standards body for professional motion-imaging engineering, and ST 2067 defines IMF CPL, OPL, track files, sidecar maps, and application profiles used for versioned masters across film, broadcast, and streaming workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Versioned master and package dependency handling
used_for: mastering and downstream deliverable transformation boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: SMPTE ST 2052-1:2010
authoritative_sources.publisher: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SMPTE ST 2052-1 is an authoritative timed-text standard for captions and subtitles associated with a given media asset, making it directly relevant to caption handoff evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: TTML Profiles for Internet Media Subtitles and Captions 1.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: W3C is the standards body for TTML and IMSC 1.1 is the authoritative Recommendation for interoperable subtitle and caption delivery applications worldwide.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Internet subtitle and caption package validation

authoritative_sources.title: FCC Moves to Upgrade TV Closed Captioning Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FCC is the U.S. regulator for broadcast and video accessibility obligations, and this order summary defines closed-caption quality standards for accuracy, synchronicity, completeness, and placement.
used_for: offline review expectations for pre-recorded programming

authoritative_sources.title: Closed Captioning of Internet Protocol-Delivered Video Programming
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FCC order is authoritative for caption file carriage in IP-delivered programming and for the mechanism by which providers and distributors identify programming that must carry captions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Caption-file transfer and distributor handoff control

authoritative_sources.title: FCC 16-17 Closed Captioning Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This FCC order allocates caption responsibilities across video programmers and distributors and is authoritative for pass-through and accountability boundaries in delivery workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: A/85, Techniques for Establishing and Maintaining Audio Loudness for Digital Television
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ATSC is the recognized standards body for digital television in the U.S., and A/85 provides authoritative loudness-control guidance for production, distribution, and postproduction monitoring.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audio QC and delivery-readiness blocker handling

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and turnover validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture picture-lock state, version or cut identifiers, destination context, prior owner, next-owner candidate, and the authoritative turnover package before any handoff proceeds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team post routing and version-state normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize editorial, sound, color, VFX, caption, QC, mastering, and delivery records so owner changes, version states, and required artifacts are explicit.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Caption, audio, QC, and delivery blocker handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve caption quality issues, loudness failures, IMF or AS-11 package mismatches, and missing metadata as blockers that require the right specialist owner rather than coordination-only closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and destination-owner acceptance
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, accepted artifact set, and remaining blocker state are explicit across mastering, delivery, and platform or broadcaster acceptance records.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: picture-lock status, cut or version identifiers, and editorial turnover packages
AAF files, XML or EDL references, reference exports, pull lists, and source timecode mappings
IMF CPLs, OPLs, track files, sidecar maps, air-ready masters, and delivery manifests
caption and subtitle files including SMPTE-TT or IMSC packages and caption QC notes
audio stems, M&E packages, loudness reports, and channel-label metadata
QC reports, reject notes, redelivery requests, and destination acceptance records
handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, version-readiness summaries, and closure confirmations
systems_of_record: editorial project repository or NLE project tracker
media asset management or production asset management system
mastering package store or IMF repository

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope for post-production coordination.
Request and validate the minimum turnover, version, caption, QC, and delivery records needed before routing.
Track stale ownership, duplicated ownership, and blocker state across editorial, finishing, accessibility, QC, mastering, and delivery teams.
Produce handoff summaries, routing notes, dependency trackers, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Picture-lock, version, caption, loudness, QC, or destination-spec records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires creative signoff, accessibility compliance approval, legal or rights interpretation, or final broadcaster or platform acceptance authority.
Public baseline and tenant or destination requirements materially differ on deliverable format, metadata, or accessibility obligations.
A QC reject, caption-quality failure, or mastering mismatch changes whether a package can move forward.
refuse: Perform picture conform, color, sound, caption repair, mastering, upload, or other execution work inside the coordination lane.
Approve creative, compliance, accessibility, or delivery acceptance decisions that belong to adjacent owners.
Close a handoff or package without destination-owner acceptance and required artifact completeness.

domain_failure_modes: A downstream handoff proceeds without picture-lock proof, a cut or version identifier, or a complete turnover package, so finishing or mix teams work from the wrong material.
CPL, track-file, caption, or delivery-manifest records conflict and the lane collapses them into a single confident answer instead of preserving the blocker.
A caption quality failure or missing timed-text package is treated as an administrative nuisance rather than an accessibility and acceptance blocker.
Loudness or audio-package failures are ignored and the lane implies technical compliance it does not own.
The lane absorbs export, caption fixing, upload, or release execution work instead of coordinating the right owner.
Generic project-coordination language obscures post-specific records such as AAF turnovers, IMF packages, timed text, QC reports, and destination manifests.
Closure is declared without proof that the correct mastering, delivery, or destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Destination-specific delivery packages vary across broadcasters, streamers, and territories
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The exact deliverable format, metadata sheet, timed-text package, and acceptance owner can materially differ by commissioning destination.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack treats destination specification retrieval as a required tenant fact and refuses confident delivery instructions when the destination package definition is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility systems of record differ across tenants and vendors
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Editorial, MAM, QC, caption, and delivery state can live in different systems depending on the tenant stack.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The public baseline is sufficient for validated workflow and boundary rules, while exact record locations are modeled as runtime retrieval dependencies.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accessibility, loudness, and metadata obligations can vary by jurisdiction or distribution channel
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Broadcast, streaming, airline, theatrical, and territorial deliveries may apply different compliance overlays and acceptance tests.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack names public baseline rules and explicitly escalates when channel-specific or jurisdiction-specific rules would change the answer.

summary: The public DPP, AMWA, SMPTE, FCC, W3C, and ATSC baseline is sufficient to define post-production-coordination-specific workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, and failure modes for a validated coordination spec pack. Remaining variability is destination- or tenant-specific and is handled through retrieval dependencies plus explicit escalation rather than unsupported synthesis.
